compiled_state.title: Implement _compact_preconditions() and snapshot-read path with retry-once in ticket-lib.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Story
0263-62ba — Migrate 4 consumers to PRECONDITIONS, add compaction on epic-closure with ordering contract

## Objective
Implement the compaction function and update `_read_latest_preconditions()` to transparently handle both flat events and the compacted snapshot format, with a retry-once on transient file-missing.

## Implementation Notes

### Files to Modify
- `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh` — add `_compact_preconditions()`, update `_read_latest_preconditions()`

### _compact_preconditions(ticket_dir, epic_id) logic
1. Enumerate all `*-PRECONDITIONS.json` files in `<ticket_dir>` (exclude `*-PRECONDITIONS-SNAPSHOT.json`)
2. Build a merged JSON payload applying LWW across composite keys (gate_name, session_id, worktree_id) per the established Story 1 contract
3. Write merged payload to a temp file `<ticket_dir>/<ts>-<uuid>-PRECONDITIONS-SNAPSHOT.json.tmp`
4. Atomic rename (mv) the temp file to final `<ticket_dir>/<ts>-<uuid>-PRECONDITIONS-SNAPSHOT.json`
5. Mark each original event file retired by appending `.retired` suffix (rename, not delete — preserve audit trail)
6. Emit: `[compact_preconditions] snapshot written: <snapshot_path>` to stderr

### _read_latest_preconditions() update
1. Check if `*-PRECONDITIONS-SNAPSHOT.json` exists in ticket dir
2. If snapshot exists AND (no live PRECONDITIONS.json remain OR snapshot timestamp >= all live events): return snapshot
3. If no snapshot or live events are newer: run existing LWW resolution across live events (unchanged behavior)
4. Retry-once contract: if any stat/read operation fails with ENOENT, sleep 50ms and retry once; on second failure, return exit 1 with diagnostic message

### Contract invariants (from preplanning)
- compaction_ordering: epic-closure validator MUST run before calling this function
- atomic-rename to publish snapshot (no partial-write reads)
- reader handles both flat events and snapshot format transparently

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `declare -f _compact_preconditions` succeeds after sourcing ticket-lib.sh
  Verify: bash -c 'source plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh && declare -f _compact_preconditions' | grep -q "_compact_preconditions"
- [ ] test_compact_preconditions_writes_snapshot passes (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-preconditions-compaction.sh test_compact_preconditions_writes_snapshot
- [ ] test_read_latest_handles_snapshot_format passes (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-preconditions-compaction.sh test_read_latest_handles_snapshot_format
- [ ] test_read_latest_retry_once_on_transient_miss passes (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-preconditions-compaction.sh test_read_latest_retry_once_on_transient_miss
- [ ] No existing ticket-lib.sh tests regress
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh 2>&1 | grep -E "PASS|FAIL" | tail -20
- [ ] `ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py
- [ ] Script is executable
  Verify: test -x plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately

comments.body:
AC amendment: .tmp file cleanup on compaction failure — The _compact_preconditions() function must clean up <ts>-<uuid>-PRECONDITIONS-SNAPSHOT.json.tmp if the atomic rename fails (filesystem error). Use a local trap or manual rm in the error path. Add acceptance criterion:
- [ ] No .tmp files left behind when _compact_preconditions() fails mid-operation
  Verify: In test_compact_preconditions_writes_snapshot, simulate a rename failure (chmod -w on target dir) and assert no *.tmp files remain after the function exits
comments.author: Test
